Secondly, you don't want to make any jokes about the mafia, not that there is such a thing, etc. And so Larry says he does the whole thing and it all was great and he's happy to be getting out of there. And the guy corners him in the back and says, you were gray or waist-thrilled, wonderful, and you did us a favor. Now we owe you a favor. And there's no, no, no. He says, no, no, no. We owe you a favor. And you got to understand, we don't like owing people favors. So what do you want? Larry says, no, I don't like it. And so this goes back and forth. And finally Larry says, he hears a question that you will probably never hear any time in your life. But the guy looks at him and says, well, got anybody you want whacked? Now, Larry says, no matter how moral a person you are, how spiritual a person you are, at the moment in time that you are asked that question by someone who clearly has the ability in order to deliver on the question, you begin making a list. Well, see, now that's the premise of an exclusive. Because as soon as you put in front of people the premise that your peers, your competitors, people in your industry, people in your community, here's a list of 22 of them who qualify. And I'm only going to take one. They look at that list and there's somebody on there they can't stand. And the thought of them having the stool is, has motivating as is their desire for the stool, if not more so. So that's why exclusives work. The bad news about exclusives is you can only sell one stool. So you got to be careful about it. I mean, if you say I'm only going to take one lawyer per area, the bad news is there's a lot more lawyers in San Diego than there are in Pocatello, Idaho. And so as soon as you take one, there's going to be 20 more you're going to be turning down and you're spacking your head. Oh, I could have had all that money. And then it takes you two years to find somebody in Pocatello, Idaho. That's the bad news. As soon as let's say I did speakers and I said I'm only going to take one speaker from each category I'm going to work with. So one motivational person, one leadership person, one sales person, one marketing person. Good news is I'd have enormous leverage. The bad news is as soon as I got one of some, if it was a motivational person, the next day there'd be 40 more wanting to buy. And I got to say no to all of them. So there's a double-edged sword to this. Some people figure out how to split the hairs. I have a client, Dennis Tubbergun, in financial services. He's now up to six different area exclusive coaching programs. And so he's splitting the hairs by what kind of financial services they're delivering, by who they're delivering them to. So he's parsing it left, right, and front of center because he's built a system to sell this type of coaching plus area exclusive. So you think about it's one of the ways to get paid more money for coaching because understand what it does. It immediately makes the chair infinitely more valuable without requiring any modifications to the chair. The value is from air alone, where the chair might have sold for $10,000 without exclusivity. It easily sells for $30,000 or $40,000 or $50,000 with exclusivity and we didn't make any modifications to the chair. The last way to get paid more money, oh, the other kind of exclusivity I want to talk about. The example I wanted to give you about project coaching. My client, Craig Proctor, who works with real estate agents, has had for a number of years a coaching program with a lot of people in it, sort of a middle swath program. I think it's 847 a month and he keeps hundreds and hundreds and hundreds of agents in it at any one given time. A few years ago he created a higher end program, his platinum group. It was originally supposed to have, I think, 10 or 12 people in it and now he's got like 50 or 60. But they are only one per area so he is doing area exclusive with them. And then they are all getting a project per year. So two years ago, and they get me doing the project with Craig, that they then get to use. So two years ago was the building of a shock and awe box, a big box for the real estate agent to deliver to someone who thought they might want to listen and sell their home. And so the platinum group gets to watch Craig and I figure it out and do it. They get to watch Craig test it and prove it and then they get the right to use it in their area. So the project is going on during the year. And so it's sort of a combination of look over your shoulder and then finished work product and an exclusive right to use. This year it was, actually I don't know what we are going to do this year. Last year it was a recruiting an inside agent system to fix that problem for them. So think about what's combined there that inflates the value dramatically. One is area exclusivity. The other is a look over your shoulder experience. And third is a work product. Fourth is now a finished thing that you get to use looping back only in your area, as well as the three coaching meetings and the calls and all of that. That adds an enormous amount of value. Now in reality it actually came about in reverse. In Craig's case it came about because he really needed to conquer the listing problem in his own real estate business and wanted the shock and awe box for himself. But when it was going to cost him $150,000 plus points in order to have me build the shock and awe box, he didn't want to write the check. So he figured out how to get everybody else to pay for it and get what he wanted. Now it actually turns out that it turned into a pretty good business and you know, it's a multi-million dollar coaching business every year. Plus every year now he gets to pick something that he wants for his own business to get built with everybody else paying for it.
